name: Pete Townshend
abstract: Peter Dennis Blandford Townshend (; born 19 May 1945) is an English musician. He is the co-founder, guitarist, keyboardist, second lead vocalist, and principal songwriter of the Who, one of the most influential rock bands of the 1960s and 1970s.

concerts.label: Roger Daltrey: A Celebration of the Music of Pete Townshend and The Who
Roger Daltrey: A Celebration of the Music of Pete Townshend and The Who

works_created.t: Tommy: We're Not Gonna Take It

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Flanigan
about_quotes.evidence: Like every other thirteen year old in 1969, I took up the guitar, because I wanted to be another Pete Townshend or Jimi Hendrix.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Flanigan interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 15, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Mose Allison
about_quotes.evidence: You have said...what did you say about Pete Townsend... You said he made “Young Man Blues” sound like an anthem.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mose Allison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Pigeons Playing Ping Pong- Jeremy Schon & Greg Ormont
about_quotes.evidence: The Who’s guitarist, Pete Townshend, was in the pit playing with the band and I was about ten years old.
about_quotes.doc_title: Pigeons Playing Ping Pong- Jeremy Schon & Greg Ormont interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, March 8, 2024
